title: ACT III
subtitle: The Ashen Wastes
rewardRelicDesc: Once per battle, when one of your units is destroyed, it is immediately revived with half its original HP.

intro.title: THE ASHEN WASTES
intro.text:
Before the Fracture, the Ashen Wastes were a mining region — coal seams, slag heaps, villages built around the work. Ordinary.

The Fracture hit differently here. The shard didn't just shatter. It burned. And whatever dark energy poured into the gap found the dead an accommodating host.

The Wastes are not quiet. The Wastes have never been quiet since.

intro.title: THE WANDERER
intro.text:
You are Jarv. Now carrying an Iron Standard that gives your units a certain military precision.

You have a deck of cards, a route into the Wastes, and the professional detachment of someone who has learned not to be surprised by anything. Including, apparently, animated dead.

You are somewhat surprised by the animated dead.

intro.title: THE ASHEN WASTES
intro.text:
The shard is a landscape of grey ash and collapsed mineshafts, lit by fires that burn without fuel and voices that carry without wind.

At its centre, the Ashwalker — a figure that predates the Fracture, that may predate the Dominion itself. It does not rule the Wastes. The Wastes simply organise themselves around it.

intro.title: YOUR MISSION
intro.text:
Cross the Wastes. Find the Ashwalker. Defeat it.

Try not to think too hard about what 'defeat' means for something that was already dead when the Fracture happened.

outro.title: THE ASHES SETTLE
outro.text:
The Ashwalker collapses inward — not destroyed, exactly. Diminished. The fires that burned without fuel go out one by one.

For the first time in three years, the Ashen Wastes are quiet. Not silent. Quiet. There is a difference.

You stand in the ash and wait to see if anything gets back up. Nothing does. You consider this a victory.

outro.title: SOULSTONE
outro.text:
In the place where the Ashwalker fell, there is a stone. Dark, smooth, warm to the touch in a way that has nothing to do with temperature.

The Wandering Scholar, when you describe it to him later, goes very still.

'That's a Soulstone,' he says. 'They were theoretical.'

You put it in your pack. It feels like it belongs there.

jarvMoods: Currently, technically, in a place that shouldn't exist.
Professionally speaking, operating outside recommended parameters.
In the occupational sense, doing something the guild specifically warned against.
Now — by any reasonable safety assessment — somewhere else.
Technically alive, which puts you in a minority out here.
jarvIntros: You have a deck of cards, a route through the Wastes that was described to you by someone who definitely survived it, and a philosophical acceptance of ambiguity.
You have a deck of cards, a map that stops updating itself after the third checkpoint, and enough tactical experience to know when you're in over your head and do it anyway.
You have a deck of cards. The Ashen Wastes have considerably more cards, and most of them are on fire.
You have a deck of cards and the specific kind of calm that comes from having run out of things to be alarmed about.
You have a deck of cards. Something in the ash is watching you. You choose to treat that as an audience.
wastesDesc: The Ashen Wastes spread across the shard like a wound that never healed — grey plains dotted with collapsed shafts, burning slag heaps, and the occasional structure that shouldn't still be standing. The silence is the worst part. Not quiet. Silent.
The Wastes are navigable if you know the routes and avoid the unstable ground. Your map marks both. Your map was also drawn three years ago, before the dead started moving the landmarks.
Grey ash in every direction, fires that burn cold, and voices that carry meaning without words. The Wastes are not hostile — they are simply indifferent to whether you survive them.
The shard smells of coal and old smoke and something older beneath both. The Wandering Scholar called it 'entropy made ambient.' You call it 'bad.'
ashwalkerDesc:
The Ashwalker has no name in any record that predates the Fracture, which suggests it either wasn't important enough to name or was important enough to not name. Neither is reassuring.

It doesn't command the dead. It simply exists, and the dead — finding something more coherent than entropy to organise around — follow.

No one knows what the Ashwalker was before the Fracture. The current theory, held by exactly one scholar who has since stopped talking about it, is that it wasn't anything before the Fracture. That the Fracture made it.

You are choosing not to find this interesting.

The Ashwalker moves through the Wastes like weather — present everywhere, localised nowhere, and arriving at inconvenient moments. The dead it commands are an afterthought. The Ashwalker itself is the problem.

You have a plan for the problem. The plan involves cards.

Old stories about the Ashwalker agree on one detail: it doesn't fight. It consumes. It converts. It uses what's already there.

You have made a professional decision to not be there when it arrives.
priorRunSummaries: The first ashfield looked exactly as you'd imagined it. Which was alarming, because you'd never been here before.
You stepped into the Wastes with the specific sensation of having already made a mistake you couldn't identify yet.
Something about the first encounter — the way the risen dead were positioned, the angle of the burning slag heaps — felt rehearsed. Like a re-run.
The Wastes felt familiar the way a recurring nightmare feels familiar: not pleasant, but navigable, and carrying a sense that you know how it ends.
priorRunOpenings: You remember — and this is the strange part — walking this ash field before. Not recently. Not clearly. But the memory is there.
The Revenant Witch looked at you before you'd said anything. 'Again?' she said. You didn't ask what she meant.
The Ashwalker, when you reached it, didn't seem surprised. It turned toward you with the deliberate patience of something that has been expecting a specific visitor.
The first risen dead you encountered stopped. Just stopped. Then stepped aside. You kept walking and chose not to investigate why.
milestoneOpenings5: The fifth approach to the Ashen Wastes. The dead have started learning your patterns. That's new.
Fifth campaign through the ash. The Revenant Witch has started making tea before you arrive. The gesture is unsettling in ways you can't articulate.
milestoneOpenings10: Ten campaigns. The Ashwalker has started addressing you by something that isn't quite your name but is clearly meant to be you.
You've crossed the Wastes ten times. The ash remembers your footprints now. They're still there from the first time.
milestoneOpenings25: Twenty-five campaigns. The risen dead at the first checkpoint step aside without being asked. You've started saying thank you.
After twenty-five runs through the Wastes, the burning slag heaps have started aligning with your route. They are helpful. This is the most unsettling thing yet.
milestoneOpenings50: On the fiftieth campaign, something is different about the ash. It's warmer. As though the Wastes have been waiting for this number.
Fifty approaches. The Ashwalker sends an escort now. You follow the escort. The escort knows the shortest route.
milestoneOpenings100:
The Ashwalker is already waiting.

Not in its usual position — at the centre of the Wastes, surrounded by the dead. Here, at the edge. Where you enter.

'I have been counting,' it says. The voice comes from everywhere and nowhere simultaneously. 'A hundred times you have come. A hundred times the fire goes out.'

The ash around it moves in a slow circle.

'I am beginning to think you might be a habit.'

midRunTemplates: The {ordinalLower} crossing of the Ashen Wastes. The dead have started recognising you. Some of them wave.
Campaign {n}. The ash is the same grey it always is. The Revenant Witch has rearranged the bones at the first checkpoint. New layout, same message.
{n} campaigns through the Wastes. The burning slag heaps are in different positions. You take this as a sign of something and refuse to determine what.
{n} times you've walked this ash field. Something in the ground shifts when you step on it. Recognition, maybe. Not welcome.
Run {n}. The Ashwalker knows you're coming. It has always known you were coming.
The {ordinalLower} time into the Wastes. The dead at the first barricade have learned to look resigned. Progress.
{n} campaigns. The Wandering Scholar's notes on the Ashen Wastes now have a dedicated section: 'What Jarv Found This Time.'

panels.title: YOUR MISSION
panels.text:
Cross the Wastes. Reach the Ashwalker. End it.

You know the route. You always know the route.

panels.title: THE WANDERER
YOUR MISSION
panels.text:
Cross the Wastes. Reach the Ashwalker. End it.

You know the route. You always know the route.

panels.title: YOUR MISSION
panels.text:
Cross the Wastes. Reach the Ashwalker.

You know how this goes. You've always known how this goes.

panels.title: YOUR MISSION
panels.text:
Cross the Wastes. Reach the Ashwalker.

You know how this goes. You've always known how this goes.

panels.title: THE FRACTURE
A MEMORY

ashfield-scouts.label: Ashfield Scouts
ashfield-scouts.description: The dead that patrol the Wastes' edge — faster than they should be, coordinated in ways the dead shouldn't be.

soul-shrine.label: Soul Shrine
soul-shrine.description: A shrine built from miners' tools, wrapped in old smoke. Something in it still listens.

eventConfig.title: The Soul Shrine
eventConfig.description: A shrine built from miners' tools — pickaxes crossed at the base, lanterns fused into its flanks with old soot. The flame inside is black. Whatever souls it was built to honour, they haven't left.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The black flame brightens briefly. Restored 14 HP.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: Ash coins fall at your feet. +16 crystals.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The flame flickers out and relights. Nothing changes.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: The shrine takes more than offered — lose 10 HP.

pools.label: Leave an offering
pools.consequence: A miner's spirit grants you another chance — +1 life.

pools.label: Speak a name into the smoke
pools.consequence: Someone answers. A common card drifts from the flame.

pools.label: Speak a name into the smoke
pools.consequence: The smoke carries a warning back. Restored 8 HP.

pools.label: Speak a name into the smoke
pools.consequence: The name echoes endlessly. Lose 10 HP from the noise.

pools.label: Speak a name into the smoke
pools.consequence: Ash falls around you like rain. +20 crystals.

pools.label: Reach into the black flame
pools.consequence: Cold, not hot. You pull out an uncommon card.

pools.label: Reach into the black flame
pools.consequence: It burns after all — lose 16 HP.

pools.label: Reach into the black flame
pools.consequence: You find a rare card waiting inside.

pools.label: Reach into the black flame
pools.consequence: +24 crystals fall from your hand when you pull it free.

pools.label: Reach into the black flame
pools.consequence: Something small and cold falls into your palm — added to inventory

bone-fire.label: Bone Fire
bone-fire.description: A fire that burns cold — but it burns, and in the Wastes that's enough. Rest here.

plague-front.label: Plague Front
plague-front.description: A mass of risen dead, spreading through the ash without direction or pause.

ash-grotto.label: Ash Grotto
ash-grotto.description: A hollow in the ash-field — sheltered from the wind, the dead, and whatever else the Wastes have decided to send today. Rest here.

risen-horde.label: Risen Horde
risen-horde.description: The Wastes' standing army — numberless, tireless, and pointed in your direction.

shadow-guard.label: Shadow Guard
shadow-guard.description: Risen soldiers who remember their discipline — the most dangerous kind of dead.

grave-merchant.label: Grave Merchant
grave-merchant.description: A merchant who decided the Wastes were a business opportunity. Somehow still alive. Thriving, even.

revenant-witch.label: Revenant Witch
revenant-witch.description: She died in the Fracture and came back wrong. She came back with plans.
bossDialogue: "You are not dead. Unusual for this neighbourhood."
"I was a healer, before. The Wastes found that funny."

cursed-altar.label: Cursed Altar
cursed-altar.description: A makeshift altar built from the bones of something large — whatever the Wastes worshipped before the Fracture gave it something better to do.

eventConfig.title: The Cursed Altar
eventConfig.description: A makeshift altar built from the bones of something large — whatever the Wastes worshipped before the Fracture gave it something better to do. Dark ichor seeps between the joints. The altar hums.

pools.label: Make a blood offering
pools.consequence: The altar accepts the price. Restored 12 HP, lose 6 HP.

pools.label: Make a blood offering
pools.consequence: Dark energy surges through you — +1 life, lose 8 HP.

pools.label: Make a blood offering
pools.consequence: The altar is satisfied. Restored 18 HP.

pools.label: Make a blood offering
pools.consequence: The altar drinks deeply. Lose 20 HP. Worth it? Unclear.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: A rare card materialises from the dark.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: The bones rattle an answer. +22 crystals.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: The bones are silent. The hum intensifies. Nothing.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: An uncommon card rises from between the ribs.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: The altar grants you one more chance — +1 life.

pools.label: Pray to the bones
pools.consequence: A bone fragment shifts loose and lands in your hand — added to inventory

pools.label: Desecrate the altar
pools.consequence: The altar shatters — +28 crystals from the gem fragments.

pools.label: Desecrate the altar
pools.consequence: A legendary card erupts from the rubble.

pools.label: Desecrate the altar
pools.consequence: The altar retaliates — lose 22 HP.

pools.label: Desecrate the altar
pools.consequence: It was already ruined. Nothing happens. You feel watched.

necrotic-guard.label: Necrotic Guard
necrotic-guard.description: The Ashwalker's personal dead — older, slower, and considerably harder to put down.

dark-hollow.label: Dark Hollow
dark-hollow.description: A collapsed mineshaft — safe, lightless, and blessedly quiet. Rest here.

bone-brigade.label: Bone Brigade
bone-brigade.description: The Ashwalker's vanguard — what remains of the miners who worked these shafts before the Fracture.

death-knight.label: Death Knight
death-knight.description: A Dominion officer who fell in the Fracture and rose again — armoured, organised, and entirely without mercy.
bossDialogue: "I remember orders. I remember duty. I remember everything."
"The Dominion is gone. My orders are not."

soul-pyre.label: Soul Pyre
soul-pyre.description: A convergence of the Wastes' necromantic energy — everything that has died here gathers at this point before the Ashwalker pulls it forward.

ashwalker.label: The Ashwalker
ashwalker.description: What the Fracture left behind when it burned through the Wastes. Not alive. Not dead. Patient.
bossDialogue: "You carry the warmth of the living. I remember what that felt like."
"The Wastes take everything eventually. I am simply the mechanism."
"You have come to end me. Many have. I am the Ashwalker. I walk the ash of those who tried."

ash-crossing.label: Ash Crossing
ash-crossing.description: The border between the living shard and the dead. The undead here are older than the Fracture.
